response.justification: While the candidate demonstrates strong software engineering fundamentals and mentoring capability, they are fundamentally misaligned with the role's core requirements. C++ expertise ended in 2014–15 (11 years ago), and recent experience is entirely web-stack focused (TypeScript, Node.js, React). The role demands expertise in industrial automation, embedded systems, networking, multi-threading, message brokers, MQTT, and IEC 62443 security standards—none of which are evident in the resume. The gap between web development and industrial C++ systems is too large to bridge for a mid-senior position.
strengths: Deep software craftsmanship skills with TDD, code quality mindset, and cross-domain learning capability
Proven mentoring and knowledge-sharing through workshops, talks, and community contributions
19 years of diverse technical experience demonstrating strong problem-solving and ownership
concerns: C++ expertise is severely outdated (last used 2014–15); recent 3+ years entirely on web stack (TypeScript, Node.js, React)
No demonstrated experience in industrial automation, embedded systems, IoT, multi-threading, or networking—core to the role
Missing expertise in security practices, IEC 62443 standards, and SDL implementation required for industrial cybersecurity role

key_factors.factor: C++ and embedded systems expertise gap
Software engineering fundamentals and code quality
Industrial automation and security domain knowledge
